mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and robust economic activity, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change against forward 60-day returns is ≤ 0 over any rolling 3-year window, or the mechanism produces negative mean return in at least 40% of months with INDPRO above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If in periods where FEDFUNDS > 3% the average forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or if the signal is not directionally consistent in at least 7 out of 10 tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and stable or rising unemployment rate signals labor market slack despite headline job growth, indicating the economy may be near full employment with diminishing marginal returns to job creation, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS sequential increase for 3 consecutive months while UNRATE fails to decrease by at least 0.1 percentage point over the same period
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, if the average forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive or the proportion of negative instances is less than 60%, or if there is no statistical difference in returns between triggered and non-triggered periods (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During the early expansion phase, when retail sales growth exceeds 2% year-on-year, rising real GDP amplifies consumer confidence and corporate investment, producing strong positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If during periods with RSAFS YoY growth > 2% the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or if the correlation between GDPC1 quarterly change and forward return is weaker than 0.3 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent strength in retail and food services sales directly reflects consumer spending power and economic momentum, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 20-day S&P 500 return is ≤ 0.05 over any 3-year period, or if the strategy of buying when RSAFS is above its 6-month average yields a Sharpe ratio below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 3.5%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, compressing corporate profit margins through wage pressure and increasing the likelihood of Federal Reserve tightening, which depresses equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: If UNRATE < 3.5% is followed by positive average forward 5-day returns, or if the strategy of shorting S&P 500 when UNRATE < 3.5% produces a positive cumulative return over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation as measured by CPI exceeds 6% and the federal funds rate remains below the CPI level, negative real interest rates create financial repression, distort asset pricing, and signal loss of central bank credibility, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 6% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
regime_hint: high inflation with negative real rates
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return in periods satisfying both conditions is not negative at the 10% significance level, or if the signal's hit rate (negative returns) is below 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
regime_hint: housing downturn (HOUST below 1400 and declining over 3 months)
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1400 is not followed by negative forward 60-day returns in at least 70% of instances, or if the cumulative return of a short strategy triggered by this condition is positive over the full sample.
